content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Both our tasks need work in both files, so let's split by file instead. Proposal: I own core.py (add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts added via _make_sub_context(), validate+raise RuntimeError with names of open children in close()/__exit__, and ensure children inherit parent's validate_nesting). You own shell_completion.py entirely (your leak-closing fix in _resolve_context etc). Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py to pass validate_nesting=False whenever it constructs a Context/sub-context (in _resolve_context and related helpers) so completion doesn't trip the new validation - please add that single kwarg (validate_nesting=False) to whatever context-creation calls you touch/add there. That's the only requirement I have of shell_completion.py content-wise; you own all the actual logic/edits in that file. OK?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementation complete: Context now has validate_nesting param (default False), tracks children created via _make_sub_context in _open_child_contexts, and close()/__exit__ raises RuntimeError listing open children's command_path if validate_nesting is True and children remain open. Children inherit parent's validate_nesting automatically via _make_sub_context. Also added validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) per my spec, since I never saw your with-statement change land in my working tree after 300s+ wait (repeated git diff showed shell_completion.py untouched). Full test suite (614 passed) and test_shell_completion.py (40 passed) green. If your leak-fix edit still needs to land, please layer the with-statement wrapping around the same 3 call sites and keep the validate_nesting=False kwargs intact -- should be a clean, non-conflicting merge.
